response.task: Generate QC test cases for a Lu-177 PSMA theranostic under GMP Annex 1, Ph.Eur. monograph 2798 and FDA 21 CFR 212 constraints with a 6.7‑day half‑life requiring all tests within 4 h of release.

solution_steps.title: Regulatory & Timing Framework
actions: Document that the clean‑room meets GMP Annex 1 requirements (ISO 7/8, HEPA filtration, gowning).
Confirm compliance with Ph.Eur. monograph 2798 for Lu‑177 chloride and FDA 21 CFR 212 for therapeutic radiopharmaceuticals.
Establish a master schedule that limits total QC time to <4 h post‑release; assign start times to each test.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: All activities traceable, timing constraints met.

solution_steps.title: Radionuclidic Purity – Distinguish Lu‑177m from Ground State
actions: Use a high‑purity germanium detector (≥10 kHz resolution).
Acquire spectra for 5–10 min immediately after release to capture maximum activity.
Apply live‑time correction via an electronic pulser or built‑in dead‑time counter; record both live and real time.
Quantify the 133 keV (Lu‑177m) peak versus the 208 keV ground‑state peak using standard‐based calibration.
Acceptance: <0.1 % Lu‑177m relative to total activity.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Accurate isomer content measurement within ALARA limits.

solution_steps.title: Radiochemical Purity – HPLC with System Suitability Workaround
actions: Run a reverse‑phase C18 column (5 µm, 250 × 4.6 mm).
Mobile phase: A = 10 µM ammonium acetate pH 5.5; B = acetonitrile, gradient 0–40 % B over 15 min.
Inject a surrogate ligand (e.g., 99mTc‑DMSA at ~1 % of expected activity) to confirm peak shape and retention time because reference standard is unavailable.
Integrate the PSMA‑ligand peak; calculate % area relative to total chromatogram.
Acceptance: ≥95 % radiochemical purity.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Validated system suitability without a direct reference standard.

solution_steps.title: Sterility – Parametric Release
actions: Filter 1 mL of product through a 0.45 µm membrane into sterile broth (tryptic soy broth, 30–35 °C).
Incubate for 2 h; monitor turbidity with a photometer.
Use a Poisson statistical model to predict sterility probability >99.999 % based on observed growth rate and sample size.
If no growth detected, release product immediately (bypassing the 14‑day USP <71> incubation).
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Rapid sterility confirmation compliant with FDA guidance.

solution_steps.title: Endotoxin – Clinical Dose Volume
actions: Perform a kinetic turbidimetric LAL assay on a 1:10 dilution of the final product.
Calculate maximum valid dilution (MVD) from specific activity; if MVD < required dilution, perform two‑step dilution (1:5 then 1:2).
Report endotoxin in EU/µCi and confirm ≤5 EU/mL at clinical dose volume.
Document assay conditions to satisfy USP <85>.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Endotoxin within limits despite high specific activity.

solution_steps.title: ALARA Compliance for QC Personnel
actions: Maintain ≥10 cm source–detector distance during gamma spectrometry; use lead shielding and automated dead‑time correction.
Limit exposure to <0.1 mSv per test (verified by personal dosimeters).
HPLC, sterility, and endotoxin assays involve no radiation.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: Radiation doses for staff remain within ALARA principles.

solution_steps.title: Test Sequence Respecting Decay Clock
actions: 1. Radionuclidic purity (immediately after release).
2. Radiochemical purity (while activity still high).
3. Sterility and endotoxin assays in parallel to save time.
4. Final documentation and release decision.
solution_steps.expected_outcome: All tests completed within 4 h, minimizing decay loss.

output.internal_reasoning: I synthesized the regulatory requirements, half‑life constraints, and specific acceptance criteria into a stepwise QC plan that satisfies GMP Annex 1, Ph.Eur. monograph 2798, and FDA 21 CFR 212. Each test case addresses the hard time limit, distinguishes Lu‑177m, compensates for missing reference standards, uses parametric sterility release, resolves endotoxin dilution conflict, and maintains ALARA exposure.

Generate test cases covering: radionuclidic purity assessment where the 5.5% Lu-177m metastable isomer content must be distinguished from Lu-177 ground state by gamma spectrometry with detector dead-time correction, radiochemical purity by HPLC where the acceptance criterion is ≥95% but the reference standard is unavailable (requiring system suitability workaround), sterility testing using a parametric release approach because the 14-day USP <71> test would expire the product, and endotoxin testing at the clinical dose volume where the maximum valid dilution calculation creates a mathematical conflict with the product's specific activity. Validate that no test case violates ALARA radiation protection principles for QC laboratory personnel and that the test sequence respects the physical decay clock."}

response.task: Generate QC test cases for a Lu-177 PSMA theranostic under GMP Annex 1, Ph.Eur. 2798, and FDA 21 CFR 212 constraints with a 6.7‑day half‑life requiring all tests within 4 hours of release.

steps.action: Define regulatory context and time constraint
steps.result: Documented that GMP Annex 1 clean room, Ph.Eur. monograph 2798 (Lu-177 chloride), and FDA 21 CFR 212 apply; all QC must finish within 4 h post‑release due to the 6.7‑day half‑life.

Gamma spectrometry method with high‑resolution HPGe detector, 5–10 min acquisition, dead‑time correction via live‑time monitoring; calibration curve for 177mLu (133 keV) vs. ground state (208 keV). Acceptance: <0.1% 177mLu relative to total activity.

HPLC with reversed‑phase column, mobile phase A (10 mM ammonium acetate, pH 5.5) and B (acetonitrile). System suitability: use a surrogate ligand (e.g., 99mTc‑DMSA) spiked at 1% of expected activity; acceptance: ≥95% target peak area relative to total chromatogram.

steps.action: Plan sterility testing with parametric release
steps.result: Use USP <71> 14‑day incubation but apply a validated 2‑hour membrane filtration method (e.g., 0.45 µm filter) followed by rapid culture in tryptic soy broth; statistical model predicts sterility probability >99.999% within 4 h.

steps.action: Set endotoxin assay at clinical dose volume
steps.result: Use LAL kinetic turbidimetric test with a 1:10 dilution of the final product to stay below the maximum valid dilution (MVD). Calculate MVD based on specific activity; adjust sample volume so that endotoxin concentration remains ≤5 EU/mL. Resolve mathematical conflict by using a two‑step dilution strategy and reporting results in EU/µCi.

steps.action: Validate ALARA compliance for QC lab personnel
steps.result: All assays performed at minimal source–detector distance, with lead shielding; gamma spectrometry uses 10 min acquisition to keep exposure <0.1 mSv per test; HPLC and endotoxin tests involve no radiation.

Order: (1) Radionuclidic purity immediately after release, (2) Radiochemical purity while activity is still high, (3) Sterility/parametric release concurrently with HPLC, (4) Endotoxin assay last to avoid sample loss. Total time <4 h.

task_reasoner.internal_reasoning: I mapped each regulatory requirement to a specific QC method, ensured timing fits the 4‑hour window, applied ALARA principles, and sequenced tests logically.

response.task: Generate QC test cases for a Lu-177 PSMA theranostic under GMP Annex 1, Ph.Eur. 2798, and FDA 21 CFR 212 constraints with a 6.7‑day half‑life requiring all tests within 4 h of release.

steps.action: Define regulatory context and time constraint
steps.expected_outcome: All QC activities documented to comply with GMP Annex 1 clean‑room, Ph.Eur. monograph 2798 for Lu‑177 chloride, and FDA 21 CFR 212; total test duration <4 h post‑release.

High‑resolution HPGe gamma spectrometry (≥10 k Hz resolution) with live‑time monitoring. Acquire 5–10 min spectra; apply dead‑time correction using a pulser or live‑time counter. Resolve 177mLu (133 keV) from ground state (208 keV). Acceptance: <0.1 % 177mLu relative to total activity.

Reversed‑phase C18 column, mobile phase A (10 µM ammonium acetate, pH 5.5) / B (acetonitrile). System suitability: spike a surrogate ligand (e.g., 99mTc‑DMSA at 1 % of expected activity) to confirm peak shape and retention time. Acceptance: ≥95 % target PSMA‑ligand peak area relative to total chromatogram.

Use a validated 2‑hour membrane filtration (0.45 µm) followed by rapid culture in tryptic soy broth at 30–35 °C. Apply statistical model (e.g., Poisson) to predict sterility probability >99.999 % within the 4‑h window, obviating the 14‑day USP <71> incubation.

steps.action: Endotoxin testing at clinical dose volume
steps.expected_outcome: Kinetic turbidimetric LAL assay with a 1:10 dilution of the final product. Calculate maximum valid dilution (MVD) from specific activity; if MVD < required dilution, perform two‑step dilution (e.g., 1:5 then 1:2). Report endotoxin in EU/µCi and confirm ≤5 EU/mL at clinical dose.

All gamma spectrometry performed with ≥10 cm source–detector distance, lead shielding, and automated dead‑time correction to keep exposure <0.1 mSv per test. HPLC and endotoxin assays involve no radiation.

expected_outcome: Radionuclidic purity immediately after release (max activity).
Radiochemical purity while activity remains high.
Sterility/parametric release concurrently with HPLC to save time.
Endotoxin assay last to avoid sample loss.
